headline: Water Tariff Reform Delayed 12th Consecutive Year
context: SPAN proposal to restructure national water tariffs delayed for 12th consecutive year since original 2014 implementation target.

cards.big: Water tariff reform delayed 12 consecutive years since 2014
cards.sub: Current tariffs cover only 60% of water treatment and distribution costs

cards.big: Average household pays RM38 monthly versus RM95 actual supply cost
cards.sub: Federal and state subsidies of RM5.8 billion annually cover the difference

cards.big: Water operators accumulated RM12 billion debt due to below-cost tariffs
cards.sub: Debt prevents investment in replacement perpetuating pipe leakage

cards.big: SPAN proposed 25% phased increase rejected by Cabinet 3 times since 2020
cards.sub: Political sensitivity ahead of elections blocked every reform attempt

cards.big: Tariff delays test whether essential service pricing can overcome political cycles
Water tariff reform delayed 12 consecutive years. Underpriced water guarantees underinvested infrastructure.
